---
title: "Tutorials"
---

The tutorials combine explanation, maintained source code, and output produced
by pySEB during the documentation build.

## Python and numerical tutorial

[Symbolic and numerical scattering with Python](pyseb_tutorial.qmd) covers:

- analytic Gaussian-polymer structures;
- symbolic expressions and vector q evaluation;
- finite-sphere Debye clouds;
- callback-defined numerical subunits;
- PDB parsing and atom parameter profiles;
- mixed numerical and analytic structures.

## C++ tutorial suite

[C++ modelling tutorials](cpp_tutorials.qmd) compile and run five C++14
programs:

1. a Gaussian diblock copolymer;
2. a sphere–polymer micelle;
3. reusable nested structures;
4. a parameter sweep written as CSV;
5. a PDB-derived Debye cloud with an attached polymer.

The displayed C++ is included directly from the compiled source files. The
terminal output beneath each program is captured from the executable during
the Quarto render.

## Before you begin

Use the [platform build guides](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki/Build-Guides)
to prepare a local pySEB installation. For terminology, reference syntax, and
the available models, see the [wiki](https://github.com/Tobionecenobi/pySEB/wiki).
